top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo Ecuador's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
62 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Granfondo Ecuador's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
Granfondo Ecuador's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo Ecuador. Train it until it feels boring.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Granfondo Ecuador's mixed terrain actually does to you.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
At Granfondo Ecuador, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Granfondo Ecuador, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Base fitness for Granfondo Ecuador. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Granfondo Ecuador’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, VO2max, and Durability to prepare you for 62 miles of mixed terrain in Salinas, Santa Elena, Ecuador.
